statement.en: A new paradox of variation: larger population sizes reduce genetic diversity in prokaryotes

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The claim contradicts established population genetics theory (larger Ne typically increases genetic diversity via reduced drift), but the abstract is too sparse to evaluate the actual argument. The title suggests a novel empirical or theoretical finding requiring peer validation. Classified as FLOWING (provisional/preprint) pending full manuscript review and independent replication.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 群が大きいほど、多様性は二重に消滅する — 希釈と淘汰の相克。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 群衆の内部で、遺伝子は互いに消し合う — 多さゆえの孤立。
